mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations expand on improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during expansion periods, with a negative or zero average forward return over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns in over 60 percent of monthly observations across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200 thousand signal robust labor demand and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter through earnings growth channels.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in more than half of the months where PAYEMS exceeds the 156000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of contracting real GDP growth and rising federal funds rates creates stagflationary pressure that suppresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 decline and FEDFUNDS increase fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample hit rate below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion without labor market overheating, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day SPX returns in over 55 percent of triggered cases during stable growth periods.
